**read only file**

GENERIC RESEARCH STUDY REQUIREMENTS CHECKLIST

=============================================

Version: 2026-06-08 | Fully generic & adaptable to ANY research field, methodology, or scale

(quantitative / qualitative / mixed / experimental / observational / computational / theoretical / lab / field / AI / social science / engineering / market research / etc.)

## INSTRUCTIONS FOR USE

- Copy this entire block into a .txt file or text editor.
- Use it as a living checklist: mark [x] when complete.
- Add field-specific sub-items as needed (e.g. lab safety, model cards, animal ethics).
- Review at every major milestone.

---

## RESEARCH DEFINITION & JUSTIFICATION

- [ ] Research problem / question clearly defined, focused, and researchable
- [ ] Primary objective(s) and secondary objective(s) are SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound)
- [ ] Hypotheses (if applicable) or exploratory aims explicitly stated
- [ ] Background, rationale, and significance documented with supporting evidence
- [ ] Literature review completed and research gap / novelty clearly identified
- [ ] Expected impact, beneficiaries, and contribution to the field articulated
- [ ] Scope, boundaries, and limitations explicitly defined (in-scope vs. out-of-scope)

## STUDY DESIGN & METHODOLOGY

- [ ] Overall research design chosen and justified (experimental, survey, case study, ethnography, simulation, etc.)
- [ ] Detailed methodology section written (procedures, interventions, measurements, observations)
- [ ] Data collection methods and instruments/tools selected and justified (validated where possible)
- [ ] Sampling strategy and sample size justification completed (power analysis or qualitative saturation)
- [ ] Inclusion / exclusion / withdrawal criteria clearly stated
- [ ] Timeline of activities / schedule of events outlined
- [ ] Alternative designs considered and rationale for chosen approach documented

## STUDY POPULATION / SUBJECTS / DATA SOURCES & SAMPLING

- [ ] Target population / data sources precisely defined
- [ ] Sampling frame and recruitment / access strategy developed
- [ ] Sample size calculation or justification completed
- [ ] Recruitment materials, screening tools, or data source selection criteria prepared
- [ ] Plan for handling non-response, attrition, or missing data outlined

## DATA COLLECTION, MANAGEMENT, ANALYSIS & REPRODUCIBILITY

- [ ] Detailed data collection plan (tools, frequency, format, responsible parties)
- [ ] Data Management Plan (DMP) created (storage, backup, security, versioning, retention)
- [ ] Data analysis plan pre-specified (statistical methods, software, qualitative coding framework)
- [ ] FAIR principles addressed (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) where applicable
- [ ] Open science / reproducibility practices planned (preregistration, open methods, open data/code, detailed materials)
- [ ] Version control system for data, code, and documents established (e.g. Git)
- [ ] Data sharing and archiving strategy defined (repository, embargo, access conditions)

## ETHICAL, LEGAL, REGULATORY & SAFETY COMPLIANCE

- [ ] Appropriate ethics / regulatory review body identified and approval obtained (or application submitted)
- [ ] Informed consent process / information sheet / waiver documented and approved
- [ ] Risks to participants / subjects / environment / society identified and minimized
- [ ] Benefits vs. risks clearly articulated and justified
- [ ] Data privacy, confidentiality, and protection measures defined (GDPR, local laws, anonymization, etc.)
- [ ] Conflict of interest and research integrity policies addressed
- [ ] Required permits, licenses, or compliance documentation secured (lab safety, biosafety, export controls, IP, etc.)
- [ ] Vulnerable populations or sensitive topics handled with extra safeguards (if applicable)

## RESOURCES, BUDGET, TEAM & FEASIBILITY

- [ ] Detailed budget prepared and approved/funded (personnel, equipment, consumables, travel, compensation, software, publication fees, contingencies)
- [ ] Funding source(s) secured or proposal submitted with realistic budget justification
- [ ] Team roles, responsibilities, and required expertise defined
- [ ] Gaps in expertise identified and addressed (collaborators, consultants, training)
- [ ] Equipment, software, facilities, and access permissions secured and validated
- [ ] Feasibility assessment completed (time, cost, technical, logistical, access)

## TIMELINE, MILESTONES & PROJECT MANAGEMENT

- [ ] Realistic project timeline / Gantt chart created with phases and dependencies
- [ ] Key milestones and deliverables defined (protocol finalization, data collection complete, analysis complete, draft manuscript, etc.)
- [ ] Project management approach chosen (agile, stage-gate, traditional, etc.)
- [ ] Communication plan for team and stakeholders established
- [ ] Progress tracking and reporting mechanism in place

## QUALITY ASSURANCE, VALIDITY, RELIABILITY & BIAS MITIGATION

- [ ] Strategies to ensure validity, reliability, and trustworthiness defined
- [ ] Blinding, randomization, or allocation concealment planned (if applicable)
- [ ] Pilot / pre-test / validation study planned (if needed)
- [ ] Bias identification and mitigation strategies documented
- [ ] Inter-rater / inter-observer reliability or calibration procedures defined (if relevant)
- [ ] Sensitivity / robustness analyses planned
- [ ] Peer review, internal audit, or advisory input scheduled

## RISK ASSESSMENT, MONITORING & CONTINGENCY PLANNING

- [ ] Comprehensive risk register created (scientific, operational, ethical, financial, external, reputational)
- [ ] Probability and impact of each risk assessed
- [ ] Mitigation strategies and contingency plans documented for high/medium risks
- [ ] Monitoring and safety reporting procedures defined (adverse events, protocol deviations, data quality)
- [ ] Early stopping rules or adaptive design elements considered (if applicable)

## DISSEMINATION, REPORTING, IMPACT & KNOWLEDGE TRANSLATION

- [ ] Target dissemination outlets identified (journals, conferences, reports, policy briefs, repositories)
- [ ] Authorship and contribution policy established (e.g. CRediT taxonomy)
- [ ] Open access / pre-print strategy defined
- [ ] Plain-language summary or stakeholder communication plan prepared (if relevant)
- [ ] Knowledge translation / impact pathway outlined
- [ ] Reporting guidelines followed (CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, or discipline-specific)

## DOCUMENTATION, ARCHIVING & POST-STUDY REQUIREMENTS

- [ ] Full research protocol / study plan documented and version-controlled
- [ ] Amendment log and change management process established
- [ ] Final report, thesis, or manuscript structure outlined
- [ ] Data, code, materials, and documentation archived in appropriate repository
- [ ] Data retention and destruction policy defined and compliant
- [ ] Lessons learned / post-project review process planned
- [ ] Future research directions or follow-up studies identified

---

END OF CHECKLIST

This checklist is derived from standard research protocol frameworks (WHO, NIH, university IRBs), open science / FAIR principles, and project management best practices. It ensures scientific rigor, feasibility, compliance, ethics, and reproducibility while remaining completely generic and customizable for any research domain.

Save this as `research_study_requirements_checklist.txt` for easy reuse.
